$/usr/lib64/python3.9/idlelib/tree.py
	listicons%
Handle scrollwheel event.
    For wheel up, event.delta = 120*n on Windows, -1*n on darwin,
    where n can be > 1 if one scrolls fast.  Flicking the wheel
    generates up to maybe 20 events with n up to 10 or more 1.
    Macs use wheel down (delta = 1*n) to scroll up, so positive
    delta means to scroll up on both systems.
    X-11 sends Control-Button-4,5 events instead.
    The widget parameter is needed so browser label bindings can pass
    the underlying canvas.
    This function depends on widget.yview to not be overridden by
    a subclass.
    r
units
break)	Z	EventTypeZ
MouseWheelZ
deltaZ
ButtonPressZ
type
widget
yviewZ
SCROLL)
eventr*
linesr"
wheel_event;

__name__
__module__
__qualname__r;
TreeItemz
Abstract class representing tree items.
    Methods should typically be overridden, otherwise a default action
    is used.
    c
z)Constructor.  Do whatever you need to do.Nr"
TreeItem.__init__c
Return text string to display.Nr"
TreeItem.GetTextc
z>Return label text string to display in front of text (if any).Nr"
TreeItem.GetLabelTextNc
%Do not override!  Called by TreeNode.N)
expandable
IsExpandablerM
TreeItem._IsExpandablec
z"Return whether there are subitems.r	
TreeItem.IsExpandablec
GetSubListr
TreeItem._GetSubListc
z-Return whether the item's text may be edited.Nr"
TreeItem.IsEditablec
z+Change the item's text (if it is editable).Nr"
TreeItem.SetTextc
z-Return name of icon to be displayed normally.Nr"
TreeItem.GetIconNamec
z2Return name of icon to be displayed when selected.Nr"
TreeItem.GetSelectedIconNamec
z%Return list of items forming sublist.Nr"
TreeItem.GetSubListc
z%Called on a double-click on the item.Nr"
TreeItem.OnDoubleClick)
